commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content:
Trees. Many translate the Hebrew, "mulberry trees "or leave the original word, becaim, "the heights of Bochim. "Septuagint seem to give a double version: "the sound of the agitation (or Alexandrian, "shutting up "(Haydock) as with an army on all sides) of the woods, of the lamentation. "(Menochius)
Theodoret supposes, "the woods put in motion, without any wind. "It is thought that an army of spirits went before David, and threw the enemy into a panic. Storms of hail, seem to have also cut them down, Isaias xxviii. 21., and Psalm xvii. 9.

methodology.step_1: Parse main page with BeautifulSoup for structure
methodology.step_3: Use Firecrawl to get full content from 'Go to Commentary' links
methodology.step_4: Fallback to BeautifulSoup for accordion content if needed
advantages: Records verses even without commentaries for complete tracking
